verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, which comprises sperm-specific and oocyte-specific proteins involved in gamete fusion during fertilization. NCBI explicitly places IZUMO4 in the nucleus, indicating a nuclear-resident localization. The IZUMO family proteins (IZUMO1, IZUMO2, IZUMO3, IZUMO4) are expressed exclusively or predominantly in the germline and function in the context of meiosis and gamete recognition, not in somatic cell surface biology. No evidence supports dual localization, cell-state-induced surfacing, or stable PM anchoring in any somatic lineage. The canonical biology is nuclear/germline-restricted, with no documented outer-leaflet PM presentation.
